steps.title: Identify the logical relationship
steps.content: The second sentence explains the technique: smothering the flame around the vessel. The third sentence states what happens to the vessel: it becomes shiny and black. The smothering causes the color change.

steps.title: Pick the transition that signals cause-and-effect
steps.content: "As a result" introduces an effect that follows from a stated cause — exactly the relationship between smothering the flame and the resulting black hue.

steps.title: Eliminate the wrong choices
steps.content:
• "On the contrary,": signals contradiction, but the third sentence does not contradict the second; it states the outcome. ✗
  • "For example,": signals an instance of a general claim, but the black hue is not an example of smothering the flame — it is its consequence. ✗
  • "Previously,": signals a prior point in time, but the black hue happens after, not before, the smothering. ✗
  • "As a result,": correctly marks the black hue as the effect of smothering the flame. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
